name: Cabeza Líjar Trail
name_original: Cabeza Líjar – circular desde Alto del León (SL-M 44001)
description: Hike the Cabeza Líjar Trail, a moderate 5.2-mile route in Sierra de Guadarrama with panoramic views and historical bunkers.
region_intro: Hiking around El Espinar offers diverse natural landscapes on the northern slope of the Sierra de Guadarrama, within the province of Segovia. The region is characterized by mountain ranges of the Sistema Central, including peaks like Cabeza Líjar (1,823 m) and Montón de Trigo (2,155 m). Rivers such as the Moros, Gudillos, and Voltoya flow through varied ecosystems, from Scots pine forests and melojo oak groves to high mountain pastures. This area provides a wide range of altitudinal gradients and ecological diversity for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around El Espinar The most popular hiking route is Cabeza Líjar Trail, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views and includes historical bunkers. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Mujer Muerta Circular Trail, a difficult 14.7 miles (23.7 km) path. This trail provides extensive views across the Sierra de Guadarrama. Local hikers also love the Arcipreste de Hita Rock and Cerro de la Sevillana Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through areas of natural beauty and historical significance, often completed in about 5 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around El Espinar is defined by mountain ranges, extensive pine forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain routes. The routes in El Espinar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore El Espinar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking difficulty levels can I expect in El Espinar?
A: El Espinar offers a wide range of hiking difficulties. You'll find options from easy strolls to challenging mountain routes. The area features over 80 routes, with 3 classified as easy, 49 as moderate, and 34 as difficult, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in El Espinar?
A: Yes, El Espinar has several easy routes suitable for families or beginners. These trails often wind through the extensive pine forests and along river valleys, providing a pleasant experience for all ages. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed here, you can filter for easy routes on komoot to find suitable options.

Q: Can I find routes with panoramic views or viewpoints in El Espinar?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain of El Espinar, part of the Sierra de Guadarrama, offers numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957433912/cabeza-lijar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Líjar Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides spectacular panoramic vistas. Additionally, the region boasts viewpoints like the "Mirador de los Buitres," known for birdwatching, and Peña del Águila.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see while hiking in El Espinar?
A: Yes, the area around El Espinar features several beautiful water features. The brook of El Boquerón has a three-meter high waterfall. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2714437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca del Asno Gorge and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5713612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducha de los Alemanes&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots for their natural beauty.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on the trails?
A: El Espinar is characterized by diverse ecosystems due to its varied altitude. You'll hike through extensive Scots pine forests, melojo oak groves, and holm oak forests. Along the rivers, you'll find riparian forests, and in higher areas, scrubland and high mountain pastures. This ecological richness supports a variety of flora, including wildflowers like narcissus and orchids.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, some trails in El Espinar combine natural beauty with historical significance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957433912/cabeza-lijar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Líjar Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes historical bunkers from the Civil War. The Ruta del Arcipreste de Hita also explores areas linked to ancient narratives, and the Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría showcases ancient engineering.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in El Espinar?
A: Yes, El Espinar offers several excellent circular routes. Popular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1230681687/la-mujer-muerta-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mujer Muerta Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which provides extensive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146740379/arcipreste-de-hita-rock-and-cerro-de-la-sevillana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcipreste de Hita Rock and Cerro de la Sevillana Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1156919239/cabeza-reina-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Reina Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in El Espinar?
A: The hiking trails in El Espinar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the extensive network of well-maintained paths, and the stunning panoramic views from the mountain peaks.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs in El Espinar?
A: Generally, hiking with dogs is permitted on most trails in El Espinar, especially given its natural environment. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to check specific regulations for trails within the Guadarrama National Park if applicable.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Espinar?
A: El Espinar is suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with wildflowers blooming in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, but higher altitude trails provide cooler conditions. Winter hiking is also possible, especially on lower trails, though some mountain routes may require appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: Are there any specific peaks I can hike to in El Espinar?
A: Yes, El Espinar is surrounded by notable peaks within the Sistema Central. You can hike to summits like Cabeza Líjar (1,823 m), La Peñota (1,945 m), Montón de Trigo (2,155 m), and Pico del Pasapán (2,001 m). These ascents often reward hikers with spectacular views of the Guadarrama mountain range.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or natural pools along the trails?
A: Yes, the Moros River, which flows through the municipality, features human-made natural swimming pools at La Panera. These are nestled within a beautiful pine forest, offering a refreshing stop during warmer months. Always check local conditions and safety guidelines before swimming.
tips: The historic Alto del León is also traditionally called Puerto de los Leones, a name it took in 1939 when the Civil War ended and which it has maintained until the year 2000, when it acquired the official name of Puerto de Guadarrama, after the Madrid municipality where it is located. , or Alto del León, for the sculpture that presides over the roundabout of the mountain pass, both names are reflected on the road sign, at 1,511 meters of altitude, one of the lowest and most accessible geographic hills in the Sierra de Guadarrama that It has been used since ancient times. The original layout of the current road was built in 1749, during the reign of Fernando VI. In the High Middle Ages, the Valathome was disputed by Arabs and Christians, and long before by Romans, and throughout the ages by merchants, travellers, pilgrims, nomadic shepherds and bandits. The Alto del León of the 21st century is a lively mountain pass between Madrid and Segovia with a restaurant famous for its barbecues and several hiking trails in the surroundings, a regular place of active leisure for hikers from the downtown area who are looking for routes with history, botanical curiosities and good panoramic views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cabeza Líjar Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied terrain, including dirt roads, forest paths, and mountain trails. You'll ascend through beautiful pine forests, encounter rocky sections, and areas with loose stones. Some parts of the ascent can be steep and stony, leading into a high-mountain ecosystem, with the summit itself primarily composed of granite.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Cabeza Líjar Trail is estimated to take around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete. However, depending on your pace, breaks, and the specific variation you choose, similar circular routes from Alto del León can range from 3.5 to 5 hours.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and historical landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is celebrated for its stunning 360-degree panoramic views from the Cabeza Líjar Summit, encompassing the three surrounding provinces of Madrid, Ávila, and Segovia, and vistas of the Sierra de Guadarrama and La Jarosa reservoir. On clear days, you might even see the Sierra de Gredos. Beyond natural beauty, the trail is rich in history, featuring numerous well-preserved bunkers, trenches, and fortifications from the Spanish Civil War, including a viewpoint built upon an old bunker at the summit.

faqs.question: Is the Cabeza Líjar Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cabeza Líjar Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and be mindful of the varied terrain which includes rocky sections and loose stones.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Cabeza Líjar Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended. Winter hikes are possible but require appropriate gear for potential snow and ice, as the trail enters a high-mountain ecosystem. Always check weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, hiking the Cabeza Líjar Trail within the Sierra de Guadarrama does not require specific permits or entrance fees for day hikes. However, it's always good practice to be aware of and respect any specific regulations or protected areas within the park, especially concerning camping, fires, or large groups.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: A common starting point for this circular route is from Alto del León (also known as Puerto de Guadarrama). There is typically parking available in this area, which serves as a popular access point for various trails in the region.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cabeza Líjar Trail intersects with several other paths. Notably, it follows sections of the GR 10 trail, which is often well-marked with white and red blazes. Other trails that share parts of this route include "De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde el Puerto de la Fuenfría a San Lorenzo del Escorial)", "Ruta Verde Guadarrama - El Escorial", and "Alamos Blancos".

faqs.question: Is the Cabeza Líjar Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Cabeza Líjar Trail is rated as "moderate" due to its 8.45 km distance and 345 meters of elevation gain. While not overly long, some sections can be steep and stony, requiring a good physical condition. Beginners with some hiking experience and appropriate footwear should be able to manage it, but those new to hiking might find it challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or refuges along the trail?
faqs.answer: The summit of Cabeza Líjar features a viewpoint built upon an old Spanish Civil War bunker, which often serves as an informal refuge. While there are no official staffed facilities directly on the trail, the starting point at Alto del León may have some amenities or nearby establishments.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for changing weather in the mountains, wear sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support. Dress in layers, even on warm days, as temperatures can drop at higher elevations. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. A trekking pole can be helpful for rocky or steep sections.
